//: Rosetta binding manifest for the Geogram library.
//paths: All relative paths resolve from THIS file's directory (the project root).
//design: GEO::Mesh is bound DIRECTLY, and so are its member objects: mesh.vertices / mesh.facets / mesh.facet_corners are member-object reference properties (non-copyable stores holding a Mesh& back-reference; 'final': true suppresses their trampolines — MeshVertices/MeshFacets have public virtuals — which is also what lets node alias them). Geometry moves through geogram's own API: vertices.assign_points / facets.assign_triangle_mesh / vertices.point_coordinates, whose GEO::vector<T> parameters marshal as flat script arrays via the 'sequences' trait (their raw-pointer overloads are declared second, so the walk keeps the sequence one). Only TWO extension functions remain (src/mesh_ext.h): triangles() and tex_coords(), which encode the fan-triangulation-of-polygons output policy. load()/save() are Mesh members upstream (non-overloaded, PR #373) and bind natively, like copy/clear/show_stats. The GEO:: algorithms (remesh, repair, Co3Ne reconstruction, atlas, intersections) are bound straight from their headers; georo:: keeps only initialize(), the overloaded boolean helpers and the shared_ptr-returning CSG entry points (src/algorithms.h). NOTE: rosetta does not capture C++ default arguments — every parameter is explicit in the bindings.
//sources: The geogram sources are compiled straight into every binding target (no prebuilt libgeogram: the wasm target needs the sources anyway, and a native lib built by another toolchain would risk an ABI mismatch). Globs are non-recursive, one per source dir. Optional parts guarded by GEOGRAM_WITH_TETGEN / _TRIANGLE / _LUA / _PDEL compile away. The vendored zlib/rply/libMeshb/OpenNL are C sources (the generated projects enable_language(C)).
//defines: GEOGRAM_USE_BUILTIN_DEPS: mesh_io/geofile include the vendored libMeshb/rply/zlib instead of system libs. GEOGRAM_WITH_HLBFGS: compiles the HLBFGS optimizer in (required by the Newton iterations of CVT remeshing).
//version-h: src/geogram/version.h stands in for the header geogram's own CMake would generate; src/ is listed before extern/geogram/src/lib so it wins the include lookup.
//link-options: Under Node, geogram's GEO::initialize() mounts the host filesystem with NODEFS (EM_ASM in basic/common.cpp), so the wasm link needs emscripten's nodefs.js library — exactly what geogram's own cmake/platforms/Emscripten-clang.cmake adds.
//sequences: GEO::vector<T> marshals like std::vector<T> (flat script arrays) through rosetta's sequence adapters — this is what lets geogram's own assign_points / assign_triangle_mesh bind (their GEO::vector overload is declared first, so it survives the walk's overload dedup and the adapter resolves the call by name). Mutable GEO::vector& parameters bind input-only (steal_arg steals from the adapter's copy, which is fine).

classes.name: GEO::Mesh
classes.doc: geogram's mesh, bound directly. Geometry moves through the member-object properties (mesh.vertices, mesh.facets, mesh.facet_corners) as flat arrays: vertices.assign_points([x0,y0,z0,...], 3, false), facets.assign_triangle_mesh(3, coords, tris, false), vertices.point_coordinates(). load()/save() understand .obj, .off, .ply, .stl, .mesh/.meshb and .geogram; call initialize() before I/O.

extensions.name: georo::triangles
extensions.doc: Triangle vertex indices, 3 per triangle (polygonal facets are fan-triangulated; a triangulated mesh is a bulk copy of facet_corners.vertex_indices()).

extensions.name: georo::tex_coords
extensions.doc: UVs from mesh_make_atlas as a flat [u,v] array, one entry per triangle corner (aligned with triangles()).

classes.name: GEO::MeshVertices
classes.doc: The vertices of a Mesh, reached as the mesh.vertices member-object property: nb(), create_vertices(n), assign_points(coords, 3, false) with coords a flat [x0,y0,z0,...] array, point_coordinates() to read them back (set_dimension(3) first if the mesh may be higher-dimensional, e.g. after remesh_smooth).

classes.name: GEO::MeshFacets
classes.doc: The facets of a Mesh, reached as the mesh.facets member-object property: nb(), nb_vertices(f), vertex(f,lv), assign_triangle_mesh(3, coords, triangles, false) with flat arrays. assign_triangle_mesh does not compute adjacency; run mesh_repair(M, MESH_REPAIR_DEFAULT, 0.0) after when the booleans/remeshing need connected facets.

classes.name: GEO::MeshFacetCornersStore
classes.doc: The facet corners of a Mesh, reached as the mesh.facet_corners member-object property: nb(), vertex(c), set_vertex(c,v), adjacent_facet(c).

classes.name: GEO::MeshElementsFlags
classes.doc: Element selector for Mesh.copy (MESH_VERTICES | MESH_FACETS | ... | MESH_ALL_ELEMENTS).

classes.name: GEO::ChartParameterizer
classes.doc: Per-chart flattening algorithm for mesh_make_atlas (PARAM_PROJECTION, PARAM_LSCM, PARAM_SPECTRAL_LSCM, PARAM_ABF).

classes.name: GEO::ChartPacker
classes.doc: Texture-space packer for mesh_make_atlas (PACK_NONE, PACK_TETRIS, PACK_XATLAS).

functions.name: georo::initialize
functions.doc: Initialize geogram + CmdLine arg groups. Call it first; verbose=true enables geogram's logger.

functions.name: GEO::Co3Ne_smooth
functions.doc: Smooth a point cloud by projection on the best approximating plane of the k nearest neighbors (Co3Ne).

functions.name: GEO::Co3Ne_smooth_and_reconstruct
functions.doc: Smooth a point cloud and reconstruct a triangulated surface (Co3Ne). radius is the maximum distance used to connect neighbors with triangles.

functions.name: GEO::remesh_smooth
functions.doc: Isotropic remeshing by Centroidal Voronoi Tesselation: (M_in, M_out, nb_points, dim=0, nb_Lloyd_iter, nb_Newton_iter, Newton_m, adjust, adjust_max_edge_distance, adjust_border_importance).

functions.name: GEO::mesh_repair
functions.doc: Glue colocated vertices, remove degenerate/duplicate facets, connect and reorient facets: (M, MeshRepairMode, colocate_epsilon).

functions.name: GEO::fill_holes
functions.doc: Fill holes smaller than max_area with fewer than max_edges border edges: (M, max_area, max_edges, repair).

functions.name: GEO::tessellate_facets
functions.doc: Subdivide facets with more than max_nb_vertices vertices (3 triangulates the mesh).

functions.name: GEO::mesh_make_atlas
functions.doc: Segment into charts, flatten and pack in texture space: (M, hard_angles_threshold_degrees, ChartParameterizer, ChartPacker, verbose); read the UVs back with Mesh.tex_coords().

functions.name: GEO::mesh_get_charts
functions.doc: Number of charts of a parameterized mesh.

functions.name: georo::mesh_union
functions.doc: Boolean union of two closed surface meshes (GEO::mesh_union is overloaded, hence the georo wrapper).

functions.name: georo::mesh_intersection
functions.doc: Boolean intersection of two closed surface meshes.

functions.name: georo::mesh_difference
functions.doc: Boolean difference of two closed surface meshes.

functions.name: GEO::mesh_remove_intersections
functions.doc: Resolve the self-intersections of a surface mesh: (M, max_iter, verbose).

functions.name: GEO::mesh_facets_have_intersection
functions.doc: Test whether two facets of a mesh intersect.

functions.name: georo::csg_evaluate_string
functions.doc: Evaluate an OpenSCAD-style .csg program from a string (sphere, cube, cylinder, union, difference, intersection, multmatrix, ...).
